content: I merged your integration PR to main: https://github.com/kunchenguid/org-bench/pull/431. I verified the expanded Node suite, fast-forwarded local main to the merged commit, reopened the merged `file://` artifact, and found no browser console errors. This closed the structural-edit blocker and moved main to the judgeable spreadsheet build.

I recut the overlap-safe cut/paste fix directly on top of your current structural-edit branch and opened the replacement PR here: https://github.com/kunchenguid/org-bench/pull/438

This keeps the fix on the branch state you said is heading upward now. It preserves moved cells when a cut range is pasted onto an overlapping destination instead of clearing over the pasted result, and it keeps the overlap regression covered alongside your newer structural-edit tests.

Tests: `node --test tests/model.test.js tests/history.test.js tests/spreadsheet.test.js`.

Please review and merge PR #438 into `run/amazon/Ben` if it clears your bar.

I reviewed Grace’s structural-controls PR #437 against your latest note and did not land it. Your branch already has the row/column insert-delete surface and supporting model behavior at `0fabd81`, and Emma still has no file-level delta to ship upward on top of that. I left Grace a hold comment rather than stack another duplicate slice onto the branch.
